summary: Matched three SysDolphin baselib RObj routines by moving HSD_RObjGetByType, the static RObjUpdateFunc callback, and HSD_RObjAnimAll out of assembly into src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c. The PR also renamed callsites from func_8037AEA8/func_8037AF58 to the recovered HSD_* names, added robj.o to the Epilogue build list, exposed HSD_RObjGetByType in robj.h, and corrected FObjData from a struct to a union so the animation callback could access the same argument as float/int/vector data.
what_changed: Removed the assembly bodies for func_8037AEA8 and func_8037AF58 from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.s after replacing them with C implementations.

Implemented HSD_RObjGetByType in robj.c. It walks an HSD_RObj linked list, checks the high flag bit indicating a typed RObj, compares flags masked by TYPE_MASK against the requested type, and optionally checks the low 28-bit subtype when subtype is nonzero.

Implemented static RObjUpdateFunc in robj.c as an HSD_AObjInterpretAnim callback. It ignores NULL objects and non-TYPE_ROBJ updates, casts the object to HSD_RObj, then sets or clears the high flag bit based on whether val.fv is at least the external double constant lbl_804DE6A0, commented as 1.75.
Added inline HSD_RObjAnim as a thin NULL-guarded wrapper around HSD_AObjInterpretAnim(robj->aobj, robj, RObjUpdateFunc).
Implemented HSD_RObjAnimAll to iterate the RObj linked list and call HSD_RObjAnim for every entry, with an early return on NULL.
Renamed assembly callsites in jobj.s, wobj.s, and lb/code_8000B074.s from func_8037AEA8 to HSD_RObjGetByType and from func_8037AF58 to HSD_RObjAnimAll.
Updated include/functions.h comment placeholders to use HSD_RObjGetByType and HSD_RObjAnimAll instead of raw func_* names.
Added HSD_RObjGetByType prototype to src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.h and included fobj.h there for FObjData used by the callback implementation.
Changed HSD_FObjData from a struct typedef to a union typedef in fobj.h, matching the callback use where the same value storage is read as val.fv, val.iv, or val.p.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
smart_moves: Recovered semantic HSD names and immediately propagated them through existing assembly callsites, making future decompilation around JObj/WObj animation and RObj lookup easier to read.
Matched the assembly’s boolean-looking high-bit test in HSD_RObjGetByType with an explicit BOOL has_type temporary rather than over-simplifying; this likely helped preserve compiler control flow.
Used a static callback for RObjUpdateFunc because it is an implementation detail passed to HSD_AObjInterpretAnim rather than an exported API.

Introduced the FObjData union type correction to support the animation callback signature cleanly; reading val.fv is evidence that FObjData represents overlapping typed payloads, not independent struct fields.
Kept HSD_RObjAnim inline while exposing only HSD_RObjAnimAll and HSD_RObjGetByType, reflecting the apparent API boundary from the matched assembly and callsites.
Moved robj.o into the Epilogue object list alongside neighboring baselib objects such as fobj.o and wobj.o, ensuring the new C translation unit participates in the matching build.
decomp_lessons: When matching animation callbacks, verify whether payload structs are actually unions; callback parameters like FObjData may be interpreted as float, int, or vector depending on animation channel type.
For baselib linked-list helpers, preserving explicit NULL early returns and simple for-loop traversal can match compact assembly patterns.
Do not rename only the function definition: update remaining asm callsites and global placeholder comments so the codebase consistently uses recovered names.
High-bit flag tests may need to be represented as explicit boolean temporaries to match generated branches even when the logic could be simplified in idiomatic C.
External floating-point constants such as lbl_804DE6A0 may need to remain extern labels until better symbol names or constant ownership are known.
Small decomp PRs can combine function matching with required header/type/build-list fixes; the type/header changes are part of the match, not incidental cleanup.
naming_conventions: Recovered HSD_RObjGetByType for former func_8037AEA8, following the HSD_RObj* baselib naming style.
Recovered HSD_RObjAnimAll for former func_8037AF58, matching neighboring animation traversal APIs such as HSD_JObjAnimAll.
Named the static animation callback RObjUpdateFunc, consistent with its local role and PR title.
Used HSD_RObj, HSD_FObjData/FObjData, TYPE_MASK, and TYPE_ROBJ names rather than address-based labels where available.
Left unresolved data symbol lbl_804DE6A0 as an extern const f64 with a comment noting its value as 1.75, avoiding an unsupported semantic name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted 87 lines of matched RObj assembly only after adding equivalent C in robj.c.
HSD_RObjGetByType mirrors the assembly: NULL check, linked-list loop, high-bit flag test, TYPE_MASK comparison, optional subtype comparison against flags & 0x0FFFFFFF, and NULL return on no match.
RObjUpdateFunc mirrors the assembly early exits for NULL obj and type != TYPE_ROBJ, then uses a floating-point compare against lbl_804DE6A0 to set bit 0x80000000 or clear it with mask 0x7fffffff.
HSD_RObjAnimAll mirrors the assembly loop that loads each RObj aobj and calls HSD_AObjInterpretAnim with the update callback before advancing through next.
Assembly callsite renames preserve branch targets and instruction encodings while replacing raw labels with recovered function names for better cross-reference quality.
The fobj.h struct-to-union change is a matching tactic: it allows FObjData val to be passed by value and accessed as val.fv in the callback without inventing separate argument types.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename lbl_804DE6A0 or replace the 1.75 constant with a symbolic name.
Search for follow-up work that exposes HSD_RObjAnim in robj.h or keeps it inline/static only.
Check later changes to FObjData/HSD_FObjData to confirm the union layout and callback signature were retained.
Find subsequent PRs decompiling func_8037AFC0 and neighboring RObj functions after this PR removed func_8037AEA8 and func_8037AF58 from assembly.
Search for additional callsites of HSD_RObjGetByType using type constants 0x10000000, 0x20000000, or 0x40000000 to recover RObj type/subtype names.
